content:
# iter-019 recommendations

## HIGH — unblock (d1)/(d3): build the tilde-stalk module-structure helper FIRST
The prover's "(d1) ready after (c)" was wrong (analogous to (c)'s under-anticipated `T` issue). (d1) and (d3) are **both** blocked on the same precise gap — and it is NOT the (a1) sheafification wall:
- `Module ↑R ((tilde Mf).presheaf.stalk x)` and `IsScalarTower ↑R (Localization.Away f) ((tilde Mf).presheaf.stalk x)` do NOT synthesize.
- Even `Module ↑(CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away f)) ((tilde Mf).presheaf.stalk x)` fails `inferInstance` in a fresh context (entangled with `ModuleCat.of`/`CommRingCat` coercions). Confirmed by 3 `lean_run_code` tests.

**Action:** blueprint + dispatch a small infra node — a helper/instance giving the tilde stalk its `Module ↑R` + `IsScalarTower ↑R (Localization.Away f)` structures in usable form (via `Module.compHom`/`RestrictScalars` over `algebraMap ↑R (Localization.Away f)`), plus a clean handle on the `Module ↑(CommRingCat.of …)` instance so `(tilde.toStalk Mf x).hom.restrictScalars ↑R` elaborates. Once it exists:
- (d1) closes as essentially `exact tildePullbackComparison_stalk_localizationTransitivity f g x.asIdeal h` (g = extendScalars structure map via `IsLocalization.tensorProduct_isLocalizedModule`; h = `(tilde.toStalk Mf x).hom`).
- (d3) then needs additionally the germ-level naturality of `α_M = tildePullbackComparison`; attempt only after (d1).
- **DO NOT re-dispatch (d1) on the bare `inferInstance`/`.restrictScalars ↑R` route — it provably fails at instance synthesis.**

## HIGH — node-4 (a)-branch is the remaining genuine wall
Frontier now exposes the (a) sub-nodes from the iter-019 effort-break:
- `lem:tilde_pullback_stalk_sheafificationStalk` (`…sheafificationStalkIso`, effort 405) — "sheafification of a module presheaf is stalkwise-iso" (Mathlib only `Type`-valued). The SOLE deep substrate gap.
- `lem:tilde_pullback_stalk_presheafPullbackStalk` (`…presheafPullbackStalkIso`, effort 475).
These are the (a1)/(a2) nodes flagged in the iter-019 plan. Grep-verify every `Tilde`/`stalk` anchor before any prover (fabrication hazard — this project has caught 2 hallucinated Tilde-stalk anchors). Likely the next effort-break target if a direct attempt walls.

## MEDIUM — `\uses` drift on node (c) (planner domain; `% NOTE` left inline)
`lem:tilde_pullback_stalk_localizationTransitivity` statement- and proof-block `\uses` both cite `mathlib:localization_localization_atPrime` + `mathlib:isLocalizedModule_uniqueness`, but the landed T-free proof uses NEITHER. Trim to the real anchors: `IsLocalization.{map_units,surj}`, `IsLocalizedModule.{surj,exists_of_eq}`, `Module.End.isUnit_iff`, `IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul`, `LinearMap.map_smul_of_tower`, `Ideal.mem_comap`, `Ideal.eq_top_of_isUnit_mem`. (lean-vs-blueprint major; my `% NOTE` is at the chapter block.)

## Reusable patterns (this iter)
- **T-free over T-parametrised localization-transitivity.** When a localization-transitivity lemma will feed a caller that can't manufacture the intermediate local ring `T` + its tower, prove the bare `IsLocalizedModule` predicate by direct 3-field construction instead of the slick `isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange` + `IsBaseChange.comp` route. Costs ~75 lines but needs only `Module R N` + `IsScalarTower R Rf N` from the caller.
- **Coe-smul ascription.** `↑s • x` (submonoid coe) is ambiguous with ring-coe smul; Lean silently picks the wrong `SMul` and `rw` fails with "did not find pattern `s • x`". Ascribe `(↑s : R)` on EVERY coe-smul in goals/haves.
- After `obtain ⟨⟨a,b⟩, hb⟩ := …surj…`, restate `hb` as a defeq `have hb' : (↑b : _) • _ = _ := hb` before rewriting (the raw form mentions `(a,b).2`).
- `Module.End.isUnit_iff` (NOT `Module.End_isUnit_iff`) for the `map_units` bijectivity reduction.
- `change`, not `show`, to retype goals (style linter).

## Do-NOT-retry
- (d1) bare `inferInstance` / `.restrictScalars ↑R` route on the tilde stalk (instance synthesis fails — needs the module-structure helper first).
- Node-4 whole `tilde_pullback_comparison_stalk` as a single decl (the iter-018 wall; stay on the (a)/(d) sub-nodes).
